signals.reasoning: This is a pure position-management exit. The live BLUR long has gone from a small winner to flat-to-negative, retaining effectively none of peak P&L, and the request flags it urgent. Even though the mechanical board still looks bullish, this specific trade has failed and should be closed.
BTC is steady but still low-edge, with a no-trade board read and only modest support from slightly positive trend. Not worth rotating into immediately after flattening a damaged long.
ETH is constructive but weakly so, and funding is still positive. The board does not offer enough edge for a fresh trade.
SOL has small positive price action, but funding remains positive and the board is still no-trade. Nothing special here.
ZEC is interesting with solid strength and acceptable liquidity, but it is already extended and still not strong enough to be a priority entry in a RED macro regime.
MERL has momentum, but liquidity is thin and spread quality is poor. Too fragile to recommend.
ZEN has decent price strength and a slightly better board profile than many midcaps, but it is still only a watchlist-quality long, not a top-tier entry.

TURBO still has one of the better squeeze-continuation profiles on the board with negative funding, strong crowding, and a bullish trade flag. Confidence is capped because it is still a thin market and macro remains RED.
VVV has strong price momentum and good enough liquidity, but the board is still neutral and funding is positive. Interesting, not best-in-class.
COMP is mildly constructive but not decisive. The setup is still mixed and spread quality is not great.
TNSR remains one of the better fresh longs with strong 24h expansion, negative funding, and a bullish trade flag. Macro still being RED keeps this at reduced-size conviction only.
